title: Gaffrey Lab Infant Microstates and Attention
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 43 infants aged 5-10 months during a passive viewing task involving relaxing videos with attention-grabbing cues. The study investigates infant neural microstates and attentional processes as part of a larger investigation into the effects of bias and discrimination on maternal health and infant neurodevelopment. High-density EEG was recorded using a 128-channel geodesic sensor net while infants watched video stimuli presented in controlled laboratory conditions.
methods_description: EEG was recorded at 1000 Hz using a 128-channel HydroCel Geodesic Sensor Net (Electrical Geodesics, Eugene, OR), referenced to vertex (Cz), with impedances maintained below 50 kΩ. Participants (43 infants, 5-10 months old) sat on caregiver's lap and viewed up to 15 minutes of relaxing videos (ten 90-second videos with breaks). Attention-grabbing cues (3-second rattle videos) preceded each video. Stimuli were presented using E-Prime software.
